goal: Maintain target visual references for each major game state so art, lighting, UI absence, and long-session content can be compared against a durable direction.

states.purpose: Baseline first-person no-HUD corridor readability target.
validationUse: Check wall/floor/ceiling material breakup and midground route cue.

states.purpose: Define how the cube/sequence objective space should look without relying on HUD instructions.
validationUse: Compare anomaly room lighting, pedestal readability, object silhouettes, and placement affordance.

states.purpose: Define staged procedural generation as an inspectable diegetic loading experience.
validationUse: Guide loading scene layout and progress stage readability.

states.purpose: Show what later-session content density should become after players have moved through the maze for longer.
validationUse: Guide cumulative content packs: region landmarks, denser props, stronger anomaly traces, and route memory cues.

branchAlignmentQuestion: Have I checked whether each reference still aligns with the original target of a readable first-person maze horror game and do I need alignment before reconciliation?
